In [1]:
# ==========================================================
# HR ATTRITION ANALYSIS
# Phase 4 : Python Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
# ==========================================================

# ===========================
# 1. Import Libraries
# ===========================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

plt.style.use("ggplot")
pd.set_option("display.max_columns", None)
In [2]:
# ===========================
# 2. Load Dataset
# ===========================

df = pd.read_excel(r"W:\Projects\HR Attrition Analysis\Dataset\hr_attrition_clean.xlsx")

# ===========================
# 3. View Dataset
# ===========================

print(df.head())
print("\nLast 5 Records")
print(df.tail())

# ===========================
# 5. Missing Values

print("\nMissing Values")
print(df.isnull().sum())

# ===========================
# 6. Duplicate Records
# ===========================

print("\nDuplicate Records")
print(df.duplicated().sum())

# ===========================
# 7. Descriptive Statistics
# ===========================

print(df.describe())
print(df.describe(include=["object", "string"]))

# ===========================
# 8. Unique Values
# ===========================

categorical = df.select_dtypes(include=["object", "string", "category"])

for col in categorical.columns:
    print("\n", col)
    print(df[col].value_counts())

In [5]:
# ==========================================================
# UNIVARIATE ANALYSIS
# ==========================================================

# Age Distribution

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
sns.histplot(df["Age"], bins=20, kde=True)
plt.title("Age Distribution")
plt.show()

# Monthly Salary

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
sns.histplot(df["Monthly_Salary"], bins=20)
plt.title("Monthly Salary Distribution")
plt.show()

# Department

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
sns.countplot(data=df, x="Department")
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.title("Department Distribution")
plt.show()

# Job Role

plt.figure(figsize=(12,5))
sns.countplot(data=df, x="Job_Role")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.title("Job Role Distribution")
plt.show()

# Gender

plt.figure(figsize=(5,5))
df["Gender"].value_counts().plot.pie(autopct="%1.1f%%")
plt.ylabel("")
plt.title("Gender Distribution")
plt.show()

# Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(5,5))
sns.countplot(data=df, x="Attrition")
plt.title("Employee Attrition")
plt.show()

# Overtime

plt.figure(figsize=(5,5))
sns.countplot(data=df, x="Overtime")
plt.title("Overtime Distribution")
plt.show()

# Work Life Balance

plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
sns.countplot(data=df, x="Work_Life_Balance")
plt.title("Work Life Balance")
plt.show()

# Job Satisfaction

plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
sns.countplot(data=df, x="Job_Satisfaction")
plt.title("Job Satisfaction")
plt.show()
In [6]:
# ==========================================================
# BIVARIATE ANALYSIS
# ==========================================================

# Department vs Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
sns.countplot(data=df,x="Department",hue="Attrition")
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.title("Department vs Attrition")
plt.show()

# Job Role vs Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(14,6))
sns.countplot(data=df,x="Job_Role",hue="Attrition")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.title("Job Role vs Attrition")
plt.show()

# Gender vs Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
sns.countplot(data=df,x="Gender",hue="Attrition")
plt.title("Gender vs Attrition")
plt.show()

# Overtime vs Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
sns.countplot(data=df,x="Overtime",hue="Attrition")
plt.title("Overtime vs Attrition")
plt.show()

# Promotion vs Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
sns.countplot(data=df,x="Promotion_Last_3_Years",hue="Attrition")
plt.title("Promotion vs Attrition")
plt.show()

# Remote Work vs Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))
sns.countplot(data=df,x="Remote_Work",hue="Attrition")
plt.title("Remote Work vs Attrition")
plt.show()

# Salary vs Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
sns.boxplot(data=df,x="Attrition",y="Monthly_Salary")
plt.title("Salary vs Attrition")
plt.show()

# Years at Company vs Attrition

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
sns.boxplot(data=df,x="Attrition",y="Years_at_Company")
plt.title("Experience vs Attrition")
plt.show()
In [7]:
# ==========================================================
# MULTIVARIATE ANALYSIS
# ==========================================================

plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
sns.boxplot(
    data=df,
    x="Department",
    y="Monthly_Salary",
    hue="Attrition"
)
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.title("Department, Salary and Attrition")
plt.show()
In [8]:
# ==========================================================
# CORRELATION ANALYSIS
# ==========================================================

numeric = df.select_dtypes(include=np.number)

plt.figure(figsize=(10,8))
sns.heatmap(
    numeric.corr(),
    annot=True,
    cmap="coolwarm",
    fmt=".2f"
)
plt.title("Correlation Heatmap")
plt.show()
In [9]:
# ==========================================================
# OUTLIER ANALYSIS
# ==========================================================

columns = [
    "Age",
    "Monthly_Salary",
    "Years_at_Company",
    "Training_Hours_Last_Year"
]

for col in columns:
    plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
    sns.boxplot(y=df[col])
    plt.title(col)
    plt.show()
In [10]:
# ==========================================================
# KPI ANALYSIS
# ==========================================================

total_employees = len(df)
employees_left = len(df[df["Attrition"]=="Yes"])
attrition_rate = employees_left/total_employees*100
average_salary = df["Monthly_Salary"].mean()
average_age = df["Age"].mean()
average_experience = df["Years_at_Company"].mean()

print("\n========== HR KPI ==========")

print("Total Employees :", total_employees)
print("Employees Left :", employees_left)
print("Attrition Rate :", round(attrition_rate,2),"%")
print("Average Salary :", round(average_salary,2))
print("Average Age :", round(average_age,2))
print("Average Experience :", round(average_experience,2))

========== HR KPI ==========
Total Employees : 10000
Employees Left : 3000
Attrition Rate : 30.0 %
Average Salary : 59350.75
Average Age : 32.62
Average Experience : 5.54
In [11]:
# ==========================================================
# Department-wise Attrition
# ==========================================================

department_attrition = (
df[df["Attrition"]=="Yes"]
.groupby("Department")
.size()
.sort_values(ascending=False)
)

print("\nDepartment-wise Attrition")
print(department_attrition)

# ==========================================================
# Job Role-wise Attrition
# ==========================================================

jobrole_attrition = (
df[df["Attrition"]=="Yes"]
.groupby("Job_Role")
.size()
.sort_values(ascending=False)
)

print("\nJob Role-wise Attrition")
print(jobrole_attrition)

# ==========================================================
# Average Salary by Department
# ==========================================================

salary = (
df.groupby("Department")["Monthly_Salary"]
.mean()
.sort_values(ascending=False)
)

print("\nAverage Salary by Department")
print(salary)

# ==========================================================
# Work-Life Balance
# ==========================================================

print(df["Work_Life_Balance"].value_counts())

# ==========================================================
# Job Satisfaction
# ==========================================================

print(df["Job_Satisfaction"].value_counts())

# ==========================================================
# Performance Rating
# ==========================================================

print(df["Performance_Rating"].value_counts())

# ==========================================================
# Final Dataset
# ==========================================================

print("\nFinal Shape :", df.shape)

print("\nEDA Completed Successfully.")
